description:
## Problem

State diagram composite states (especially non-leaf composites like Processing) are sized differently from mermaid's reference implementation.

**Example from state_complex.mmd:**
- Processing: reference 191x673, selkie 128x529 (width 33% diff, height 21% diff)
- Executing: reference 121.875x394, selkie 100.8x301.8

## Root Cause

Mermaid uses `getBBox()` to measure actual rendered SVG dimensions, which includes:
- Actual font metrics for text sizing
- Visual padding and margins

Selkie uses character-based estimates which are narrower than actual rendered widths.

## Current Mitigation

Leaf composites (no nested composites) get 1.5x width expansion to approximate mermaid's getBBox() behavior.

## Further Investigation Needed

1. Measure actual font metrics differences between mermaid and selkie
2. Consider using browser-based rendering to get accurate getBBox() measurements
3. Investigate if dagre/elkjs layout algorithm differences contribute to sizing
4. Check if composite padding/margin calculations differ

## Related Files

- `src/render/state.rs` - State diagram renderer with composite sizing logic
- `reference-implementations/mermaid/packages/mermaid/src/diagrams/state/shapes.js` - Mermaid composite sizing
title: State diagram composite sizing differs from mermaid reference
